claim: LLMs are non-deterministic; running the same prompt multiple times on complex tasks yields different results, making parallel execution of multiple agents a strategy to hedge against failure and find optimal solutions
actionable: Run complex tasks with 3+ parallel agents on separate git worktrees, then merge the best outcome rather than settling for a single execution
source_title: Claude 4 ADVANCED AI Coding: How I PARALLELIZE Claude Code with Git Worktrees

claim: Git worktrees enable isolated parallel agentic coding workflows by cloning the entire codebase into separate branches, allowing multiple agents to work simultaneously without merge conflicts
actionable: Use 'git worktree add <path> <branch>' to create isolated copies of your codebase for parallel agent execution
source_title: Claude 4 ADVANCED AI Coding: How I PARALLELIZE Claude Code with Git Worktrees

claim: Well-written plans are prerequisite for parallel agentic workflows; iterative prompting is unsuitable for parallelization because each agent needs a clear, complete specification
actionable: Write detailed specs before spawning parallel agents; don't use parallel workflows for exploratory tasks requiring iterative feedback
source_title: Claude 4 ADVANCED AI Coding: How I PARALLELIZE Claude Code with Git Worktrees

claim: For UI work and ambiguous tasks, parallel agents are most valuable because multiple acceptable solutions exist, allowing selection of the best design
actionable: Use parallel agents for UI rewrites, design choices, and feature implementations where multiple valid approaches exist
source_title: Claude 4 ADVANCED AI Coding: How I PARALLELIZE Claude Code with Git Worktrees

claim: Principal AI Coding requires mastering context engineering, model selection, and prompt engineering; these fundamentals apply across all agentic tools and enable rapid adaptation to new tools
actionable: Invest in understanding context, model, and prompt design deeply; this transfers to any agentic coding tool you use
source_title: Claude 4 ADVANCED AI Coding: How I PARALLELIZE Claude Code with Git Worktrees

claim: When parallelizing agentic tasks, it's important to have enough work to justify separate branches and agent overhead; trivial changes should not spawn parallel agents
actionable: Only parallelize when the feature involves substantial work or significant ambiguity; skip parallelization for small, straightforward fixes
source_title: Claude 4 ADVANCED AI Coding: How I PARALLELIZE Claude Code with Git Worktrees
